extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won stage 7 sprint after launching from 300m, holding off GAVIRIA and BENNETT in tight finish
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step isolated Gaviria by pushing through physically with 3km remaining, forcing him to ride without leadout support.
Orica-Scott lost Ewan's leadout with 300m remaining, forcing him to launch early yet still successful against organized trains.
Corin attempted solo break at 18.7km mirroring Pellberg's stage-1 tactics, but closed by high pace from sprinter teams.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: won despite losing final leadout at 300m, showing superior raw sprint speed when launched early
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Quick-Step couldn't protect him through physical battles at 3km, forced to sprint without positioning help
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: finished third but commentary suggests he had cleaner leadout than winner, indicating speed deficit
rider_signals.CORIN Christian: neutral: attempted late breakaway at 18.7km as planned tactic, demonstrating Cannondale-Drapac willingness to gamble
